Organisers of the upcoming Paris Olympics are acutely aware that ripped bodies, high emotions and shared athlete headquarters are a recipe for sexual energy.

Lifting the COVID-induced intimacy ban that plagued the Tokyo Games in 2021, powerbrokers have announced a generous supply of 300,000 condoms to ensure their 9000 hosted athletes take safe sex precautions.

Games chiefs toldSky Newsthey planned to reunite competitors in the city of love, providing enough prophylactics for each athlete to get back on the horse twice a day.

“It is very important that the conviviality here is something big,” Laurent Michaud, director of the village told the publication.

“Working with the athletes commission, we wanted to create some places where the athletes would feel very enthusiastic and comfortable.”

Beds in athlete’s living quarters are reportedly built to hold up to 250kg — a single weight-lifter, or a couple of sprinters.

A Village Club has been created to encourage mixing across nations once again — an aphrodisiac sure to rev up competitors along with a few other French staples.

“We will have more than 350 metres of buffet with the world food... and I’m sure that the athletes will be very happy to have some French specialties made over here,” Mr Michaud said.

“But the variety will first respond to the athletes’ needs for their nutrition and their performance.”

Fuelling their will to win, the variety in both food options and nationalities will likely also spur on their sex drive.

The Olympic Village is said to be the single most expensive project at the Paris Games at $3.3 billion — largely funded by property investors.

News

Split by the River Seine in the previously rundown area of Saint-Denis, the village is described as roughly the size of 70 soccer pitches.

The Paris 2024 Olympic and Paralympic Games will take place from July 26 to August 11.

title: Athletes in Paris’ Olympic village to be provided with 300k condoms in intimacy ban at games lifted | PerthNow
description: How do Olympics organisers prepare to host thousands of ripped, sexually driven athletes? With all the condoms they could ever need!
